focus: correctness: concurrency: worker-pool cap, salvage/merge races, signal propagation

title: Fan-out timeout and stop paths do not reliably terminate lineage subprocesses
detail: runLineageProcess marks a timeout and sends SIGTERM, but it resolves only when the child emits close; there is no grace timer, SIGKILL escalation, process-group kill, or fallback resolution. The external SIGINT/SIGTERM handler also exits immediately after sending one signal to active children and writing a partial summary, so a child that ignores or delays SIGTERM can keep writing after the parent reports stopped.
claim: Fan-out lineages are not reliably terminated on timeout or parent signal, so normal timeout/interrupt handling can hang the runner or leave orphan writers racing later merge/resume steps.
counterevidenceSought: Checked fanout-run.cjs for a second kill timer, SIGKILL, process-group kill, or a close-wait in the signal handler; none is present. The sibling executor-audit.ts path does implement detached process groups plus SIGTERM-to-SIGKILL escalation, but fanout-run does not use it.
alternativeExplanation: Supported CLI executors may usually exit promptly on SIGTERM, but the code does not enforce that assumption and does not handle descendants or delayed signal handling.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if fanout-run is only invoked under an outer supervisor that kills the entire process tree and never relies on runLineageProcess timeout completion or stopped summaries.
suggestedFix: Run lineage subprocesses in a killable process group where supported, add a timeout grace period that escalates SIGTERM to SIGKILL, and make the signal-stop path wait for child close or record explicit forced-kill/orphan state before exiting.

title: Pool abort-requeue lag action is not reachable from fanout-run
detail: fanout-pool implements lagCeilingAction=abort-requeue and has unit coverage for it, but fanout-run passes only lagCeilingMs into runCappedPool and the fanout config schema exposes no lagCeilingAction field. The production runner therefore cannot activate the tested abort/requeue path.
claim: The pool API and runner/config contract have drifted: abort-requeue behavior is tested in isolation but dead through the real fanout-run entrypoint.
counterevidenceSought: Checked fanoutConfigSchema and fanout-run's runCappedPool call for a lagCeilingAction passthrough; only lagCeilingMs is wired.
alternativeExplanation: Abort-requeue may be reserved for direct module consumers rather than CLI fanout; if so, the public runner contract should say the CLI only warns on lag ceiling.
downgradeTrigger: Drop if abort-requeue is intentionally private test-only scaffolding or another production wrapper passes lagCeilingAction directly to runCappedPool outside fanout-run.
suggestedFix: Either add lagCeilingAction to fanoutConfigSchema and pass it through from fanout-run, or remove/de-scope the abort-requeue branch from production-facing pool tests and docs until wired.

title: Salvage writes the same recovered stdout to every missing iteration file
detail: runSalvageSweep computes one recoveredText value, then writes that same blob to each missing iteration-N.md discovered from the state log. Tests cover one missing iteration and mixed present/missing cases, but not multiple missing files. A lineage with two missing iteration files can get two distinct filenames containing identical non-iteration-specific recovered output.
claim: The salvage path can create misleading per-iteration artifacts when more than one iteration file is missing, because it does not attribute recovered stdout to individual iterations.
counterevidenceSought: Checked fanout-merge and found it merges registries/state rather than reading salvaged iteration markdown, so the immediate merge corruption risk is lower; the remaining issue is artifact correctness and operator trust.
alternativeExplanation: The recovered stdout may intentionally be a full transcript and therefore copied as best-effort evidence, but the produced filenames imply iteration-specific content.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade or drop if iteration markdown is explicitly non-authoritative during salvage and downstream tools/users are documented to ignore salvaged content except as raw transcript evidence.
suggestedFix: Parse stdout into iteration-specific sections before writing multiple files, or write explicit failed-marker placeholders for multi-missing cases when recovered content cannot be attributed to one iteration.
